text.title: django-base-model Core Rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules for a Django abstract base-model hierarchy.

rule.statement: Keep auto-increment `id` as the primary key. Add a separate `uid = UUIDField(default=uuid.uuid4, unique=True, editable=False, db_index=True)` for external exposure. NEVER swap the PK to UUID retroactively.
rule.rationale: Swapping PK invalidates every foreign key and breaks every migration. The dual-field pattern keeps internal joins fast (4-byte int) and external IDs opaque (UUID).

rule.statement: BaseModel is `abstract = True` and inherits `models.Model` plus the chosen mixins. Concrete models inherit from BaseModel — never from `models.Model` directly when the base exists.
rule.rationale: Abstract base classes get inlined into each child table; no extra join, no surprise multi-table inheritance. Direct inheritance from models.Model in a project that has a base is a code smell.

rule.statement: When a SoftDeleteMixin-enabled model has any `unique=True` field, that uniqueness MUST be re-expressed as `UniqueConstraint(fields=[...], condition=Q(deleted_at__isnull=True))`. Plain unique=True is forbidden on soft-deletable models.
rule.rationale: A soft-deleted row still occupies the unique index. Restoring or creating a row with the same value fails with IntegrityError. Partial index scopes uniqueness to live rows only.

rule.statement: SoftDeleteMixin overrides both the model's `delete()` AND the queryset's `delete()`. The custom Manager wraps a SoftDeleteQuerySet whose delete() sets `deleted_at = timezone.now()` for every matched row.
rule.rationale: QuerySet.delete() bypasses Model.delete(). Without a queryset override, `Foo.objects.filter(...).delete()` hard-deletes despite the model override.

rule.statement: The default `objects = SoftDeleteManager()` filters out soft-deleted rows. A second manager `all_objects = models.Manager()` MUST be defined to give admin / loaddata / migrations access to every row.
rule.rationale: Without all_objects, admin can't show deleted rows, migrations can't rewrite them, fixtures can't reload them. The pair is mandatory.

rule.statement: For every ForeignKey or OneToOneField on a soft-deletable model, on_delete is explicitly chosen from {CASCADE, SET_NULL, PROTECT, DO_NOTHING} with a comment naming the reason. Default CASCADE through soft-delete is forbidden.
rule.rationale: CASCADE hard-deletes children even when the parent is only soft-deleted; data leaks the soft-delete contract. PROTECT or SET_NULL are usually correct for child rows.

rule.statement: TenantAwareModel access from Celery tasks / management commands MUST use `with tenant_context(tenant): …`. Reading from thread-local-only middleware state outside HTTP is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Middleware sets thread-local at request time. Celery worker threads do not inherit that context; without explicit tenant_context, queries return cross-tenant rows.
